column_description: mileage of the car in miles per gallon
value_description: commonsense evidence: The car with higher mileage is more fuel-efficient.

column_description: the number of cylinders present in the car

value_description: commonsense evidence: sweep volume = displacement / no_of cylinders

column_description: horse power associated with the car
value_description: commonsense evidence: horse power is the metric used to indicate the power produced by a car's engine - the higher the number, the more power is sent to the wheels and, in theory, the faster it will go.

column_description: weight of the car in lbs
value_description: commonsense evidence: A bigger, heavier vehicle provides better crash protection than a smaller

column_description: acceleration of the car in miles per squared hour
